vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely unsupported by visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent or non-visible. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is represented only by a single, isolated stem/petiole fragment.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No identifiable mint foliage present.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Critical Status: Only a single green petiole/stem remains; no leaf lamina visible.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No identifiable silver guest foliage present.

plant_audit.P3: Single green stem fragment visible in black pot; appears to be the sole survivor of the Pothos.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears dark and potentially waterlogged or highly organic; no surface cracking observed.
biome_observations.debris: White particulate matter (likely perlite or crushed eggshell) present in P3 pot.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no significant fungal growth or external debris.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-2: No significant change; the single stem in P3 remains static.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_t-1: Lighting shift to low-spectrum/blue-heavy; no structural change in plant matter.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Return to standard LED spectrum; P3 stem remains in a state of stasis/postural collapse.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of near-total collapse. The lack of foliage across all pots suggests severe neglect, environmental stress, or senescence. The single stem in P3 is the only remaining biological indicator, and its lack of development suggests it is failing to thrive.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of white particulate matter in P3 is noted as a potential soil amendment (perlite) or intervention (eggshells).
vision_report.narrative_description: I first performed a visual scan of the pots to identify any green matter, then cross-referenced the findings with the provided registry. My validation confirms that the registry is outdated; the biome has suffered a near-total loss of specimens. The current state is characterized by bare soil and a single, struggling Pothos stem. The environment appears stable but biologically depleted.
